notes: Phase 1 seed. Every level leads to practice and proof. Levels are fixed: 1 Try it, 2 Repeat it, 3 Explain it, 4 Apply it, 5 Help someone else with it. Trust is earned through completed practice, submitted proof, useful feedback, and helping others. AI supports preparation and reflection only; it never decides a member's worth, identity, confidence, or final trust.

directions.description: Practice speaking and sharing in a way that feels clear and steady, so your ideas come across the way you mean them.

skills.description: Say what you mean in a way that is easy to follow.

levels.mastery_goal: Say one short idea out loud and record it.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick one thing you did today. Record a 30-second voice note explaining what it was and why it mattered.
levels.proof_requirement: Upload a 30-second audio clip of you explaining one thing.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what the person was talking about?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they record a real attempt instead of skipping it?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was there one idea you could follow?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name one moment that was easy to understand.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me pick one simple thing from my day to talk about for 30 seconds. Ask me one question to get started.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I just recorded my first clip. Ask me how it felt and what one word I would keep.
levels.next_step: Try recording the same idea again, a little slower.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: This clip is just for practice. You can delete it anytime, and rough first tries are expected.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A scripted clip read from someone else's words, or a clip with no audible idea, does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Record the same kind of clear idea three days in a row.
levels.practice_prompt: For three short sessions, record a 30-second clip explaining one idea each time. Try to start with the main point first.
levels.proof_requirement: Upload three short clips made on different days.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Did each clip start with a clear main point?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they show up across more than one day?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was each clip easy to follow on its own?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out which clip was the clearest and why.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan three small topics I can each explain in 30 seconds this week.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I recorded three clips. Ask me what felt easier the third time than the first.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why your clearest clip worked.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across more than one day.
levels.safety_note: Days do not need to be perfect or in a row. Coming back at all is the win here.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Three copies of the same recording, or three clips made in one sitting, do not count as repeated practice.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain in your own words what makes speaking clear.
levels.practice_prompt: Write a short note (4-6 sentences) explaining one habit that helps you speak clearly, using an example from your own clips.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short written explanation with one real example.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the explanation easy to read and follow?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example from their own practice?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn one thing from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most helpful sentence in the note.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me three questions that help me notice what makes my clearest clip clear.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I wrote my explanation. Ask me whether I would understand it if I were brand new to this.
levels.next_step: Try using this habit in a real conversation next.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: There is no single right answer. Your honest explanation is what matters.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Copy-pasted definitions or AI-written text with no personal example do not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use clear speaking in one real situation.
levels.practice_prompt: In a real conversation, meeting, or message this week, make your main point first. Afterward, note what you said and how it landed.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short reflection describing the real situation and what happened.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you picture the real situation they described?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they actually use the skill in real life?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they notice the effect of speaking clearly?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest one situation to try it in again.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me rehearse my main point for a real conversation coming up. Keep it short.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tried it in real life. Ask me what I would keep and what I would change next time.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone else start speaking clearly.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: It is fine if the conversation did not go perfectly. Trying it in real life is the point.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: An imagined or pretend scenario, with no real conversation, does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help another member make one idea clearer.
levels.practice_prompt: Find a member's early clip or note and give them one kind, specific tip that helped you speak more clearly.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a piece of feedback you gave to another member.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the feedback easy for the other person to understand?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the other person's actual work?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the other person act on the tip?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether the tip was kind as well as specific.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn a tip that worked for me into kind, specific feedback for a beginner.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I gave feedback to someone. Ask me whether it was kind, specific, and useful.
levels.next_step: Keep supporting newer members as they start out.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Helping means encouraging, not correcting harshly. Lead with what worked.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Generic praise like 'nice job' with no specific, usable tip does not count as helping.

skills.description: Notice nervous feelings and speak anyway, in small steady steps.

levels.mastery_goal: Name what nervousness feels like for you.
levels.practice_prompt: Think of a time you felt nervous to speak. Write 3-4 sentences about what you noticed in your body or thoughts.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short note describing your nervous signals.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you understand what they noticed?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they share something real, even if small?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was there one honest detail in it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back one signal they named.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me gentle questions to help me notice how nervousness shows up for me.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I wrote down my nervous signals. Ask me which one shows up the most.
levels.next_step: Try one calming breath before your next recording.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Feeling nervous is normal and human. You only share what you are comfortable sharing.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Saying 'I'm never nervous' with no reflection does not count as trying the practice.

levels.mastery_goal: Use one calming step before speaking, a few times.
levels.practice_prompt: Before three short recordings, take one slow breath or a short pause first. Record a clip each time anyway.
levels.proof_requirement: Upload three clips, each made after a calming step.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they keep going across more than one try?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the calming step seem to help at all?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out a moment that sounded steady.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Suggest two simple calming steps I can try before recording.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I used a calming step three times. Ask me if it changed how I felt.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining what calms you and why.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across more than one try.
levels.safety_note: If a topic feels too stressful, pick an easier one. Steady beats scary.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Skipping the calming step or recording all clips with no pause does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain one way you handle nervousness.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences explaining one thing that helps you speak even when nervous, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short written explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the explanation easy to follow?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real personal example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a nervous beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most reassuring part of the note.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions to help me describe what helps me feel steadier.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained my calming method. Ask me if it would help someone as nervous as I was.
levels.next_step: Use your calming step in a real, slightly bigger moment.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your method does not need to work every time. Honesty helps others most.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Generic advice copied from elsewhere with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Speak in a real moment despite feeling nervous.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick one real moment this week where you usually stay quiet. Speak up using your calming step, then write what happened.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on a real moment you spoke up.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you understand the real situation?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they take a real, brave step?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they notice how the calming step helped?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest a next moment to practice in.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me prepare one sentence to say in a real moment I usually avoid.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I spoke up in real life. Ask me what I am proud of and what felt hard.
levels.next_step: Get ready to encourage someone else who feels nervous.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Pick a safe, low-stakes moment. You are building courage, not testing it.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: An imagined moment with no real attempt to speak up does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Encourage a nervous member to take one step.
levels.practice_prompt: Reply to a member who shared feeling nervous. Offer one kind, specific encouragement that helped you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the encouraging feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the message easy to understand?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they respond to the person's real situation?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the person use the encouragement?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it felt warm and respectful.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me write a short, kind message to encourage a nervous beginner.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I encouraged someone. Ask me whether my words were kind and specific.
levels.next_step: Keep welcoming new members who feel nervous.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Encourage, do not pressure. People move at their own pace.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Telling someone to 'just relax' with no specific support does not count as helping.

skills.description: Show people you heard them before you respond.

levels.mastery_goal: Repeat back one thing someone said.
levels.practice_prompt: In one conversation today, repeat back the main point the other person made before you reply. Write down what you said.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short note with the sentence you used to repeat back.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what they repeated back?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they actually try it in a conversation?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the repeat-back capture the other point?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name one phrase that showed listening.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Give me one simple phrase I can use to repeat back what someone said.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tried repeating back. Ask me how the other person reacted.
levels.next_step: Try repeating back in a couple more conversations.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: You do not need to share private details of the conversation, just your phrase.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Describing listening in theory without trying it in a real conversation does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Practice repeating back across several conversations.
levels.practice_prompt: In three different conversations, repeat back the main point before replying. Note each time how it went.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit short notes from three conversations.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Were the three notes easy to follow?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they practice across different conversations?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did repeating back help the talk?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out the smoothest example.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan three everyday chats where I can practice listening.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I practiced listening three times. Ask me what got easier.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why listening first helps.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across more than one conversation.
levels.safety_note: Keep notes about your own actions, not other people's private words.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Three notes about the same single conversation do not count as repeated practice.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain why listening first helps a conversation.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences explaining how repeating back changes a conversation, using one of your own examples.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short written explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn the value of listening?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the clearest point made.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how listening changed one of my conversations.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained why listening helps. Ask me if a beginner would be convinced.
levels.next_step: Use active listening in a harder conversation.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your view of why listening helps is valid, even if it is simple.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A definition with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use listening in a real disagreement or hard talk.
levels.practice_prompt: In a real moment of disagreement or tension this week, repeat back the other person's view before sharing yours. Reflect on what changed.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on a real, harder conversation.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use listening in a real, harder moment?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did listening change the outcome?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest where to try this again.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me prepare to repeat back someone's view in a tense conversation.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I listened during a hard talk. Ask me what felt different from usual.
levels.next_step: Get ready to coach someone on listening.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Choose a safe disagreement. Step away from any conversation that feels unsafe.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A made-up disagreement with no real conversation does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member practice listening better.
levels.practice_prompt: Give a member one kind, specific tip on how to repeat back what they hear, based on what worked for you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the tip easy to understand?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's real work?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the member use the tip?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was kind and specific.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my listening habit into a tip for a beginner.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I gave a listening tip. Ask me if it was kind, specific, and useful.
levels.next_step: Keep helping members listen better.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Offer a tip, not a lecture. Respect how the other person communicates.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Vague praise with no usable listening tip does not count.

skills.description: Say what you think clearly and respectfully, even when it differs from others.

levels.mastery_goal: State one honest opinion in a sentence.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick a low-stakes topic (a movie, a meal, a tool). Write one sentence starting with 'I think...' and one reason.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit one opinion sentence with a reason.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the opinion easy to understand?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they share a real opinion?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the reason support the opinion?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back their main point.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn something I like or dislike into one clear opinion sentence.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I shared one opinion. Ask me if it felt true to what I think.
levels.next_step: Try sharing opinions on a few more topics.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Start with light topics. There is no wrong opinion here.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Repeating someone else's opinion as your own does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Share opinions with reasons a few times.
levels.practice_prompt: Share three short opinions on three different topics, each with one reason. Use 'I think... because...'.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit three opinion-with-reason statements.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Were all three easy to follow?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they cover different topics?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did each reason fit its opinion?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out the most convincing one.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Suggest three everyday topics I can form a quick opinion on.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I shared three opinions. Ask me which felt easiest to back up.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining how you form an opinion.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across several topics.
levels.safety_note: Disagreeing with popular views is fine. Stay respectful, not harsh.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Three opinions with no reasons do not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain how you share an opinion respectfully.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how you share an opinion without putting others down, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn to be respectful?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most respectful phrasing.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how I keep opinions respectful.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained respectful opinions. Ask me if my own examples match it.
levels.next_step: Share an opinion in a real group or discussion.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Respectful does not mean silent. You can disagree and be kind.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: An explanation that promotes putting others down does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Share an opinion in a real group setting.
levels.practice_prompt: In a real discussion this week, share your honest opinion with a reason, even if others disagree. Reflect on how it went.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on a real discussion.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they share a real opinion in real life?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they stay respectful while honest?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest a setting to try next.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me prepare to share my opinion in a group where people may disagree.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I shared my opinion in a group. Ask me what felt good and what was hard.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone else find their voice.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Pick a safe group. You never have to share opinions where you feel unsafe.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: An imagined discussion with no real sharing does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member share their opinion with confidence.
levels.practice_prompt: Encourage a member who is unsure about an opinion. Give one kind, specific tip to help them say it clearly and respectfully.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's real post?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the member act on it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it built the person up.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me encourage a member to share their opinion clearly and kindly.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone share an opinion. Ask me if I was kind and specific.
levels.next_step: Keep helping quiet members find their voice.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Support their opinion, even if you disagree with it. Help the voice, not just the view.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Pushing your own opinion onto them does not count as helping.

directions.description: Build the habit of starting, continuing, and coming back, so progress keeps moving even when motivation dips.

skills.description: Make the first step so small it is easy to begin.

levels.mastery_goal: Do one two-minute version of a task.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick something you have been putting off. Do a two-minute version of it now, then note what you did.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short note describing the two-minute action you took.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what they did?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they actually take a real action?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was it a true small start?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Encourage one more small start.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me shrink a task I am avoiding into a two-minute first step.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I did a two-minute start. Ask me how it felt to begin.
levels.next_step: Try a small start on two more days.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Small really is enough. The goal is to begin, not to finish.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Planning to start later, with no action taken, does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Take a small start on three separate days.
levels.practice_prompt: On three different days, do a two-minute start on something. Note each one.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit three short notes from three different days.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Were the notes easy to follow?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they show up on more than one day?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Were these real small starts?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out their steadiest day.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me pick three small starts I could realistically do this week.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I started small three times. Ask me what made it easier to begin.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why small starts work.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across more than one day.
levels.safety_note: Missing a day is not failure. Just start small again next time.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Three small starts on the same day do not count as repeated practice.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain why starting small helps you keep going.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on why small starts work for you, using a real example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most helpful insight.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about why small starts helped me begin.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained why small starts work. Ask me if it matches my own experience.
levels.next_step: Use a small start on a bigger goal that matters to you.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your reasons are valid even if they are simple.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A generic tip with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use a small start to move a goal that matters.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick a goal you care about. Use small starts for several days, then reflect on the progress you made.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection showing real progress on a goal.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you see the goal and the progress?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they apply it to something real?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did small starts move the goal forward?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest the next small start.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me break a goal I care about into a chain of small starts.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I used small starts on a real goal. Ask me how far I got.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone who feels stuck.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Progress can be slow and still count. Compare yourself only to where you began.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A goal you only thought about, with no real starts, does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member take their first small step.
levels.practice_prompt: Find a member who feels stuck. Suggest one specific two-minute start they could take.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the suggestion you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the person's real situation?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the step small and doable?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was encouraging.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my small-start habit into a tip for someone who feels stuck.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone start small. Ask me if my tip was kind and specific.
levels.next_step: Keep helping members get unstuck.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Suggest, do not pressure. Let them choose their own first step.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Telling someone to 'just do it' with no concrete small step does not count.

skills.description: Show up regularly in a way that fits your real life.

levels.mastery_goal: Do one small action and mark it done.
levels.practice_prompt: Choose one tiny daily action. Do it once today and write it down.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a note of the action you completed today.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what they did?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they complete a real action?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was it a clear, repeatable action?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Encourage them to repeat it tomorrow.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me pick one tiny action I could repeat most days.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I did my action once. Ask me if it felt doable to repeat.
levels.next_step: Try the same action across a few more days.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Pick something small enough to keep. This is not about being perfect.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Choosing an action but not doing it does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Repeat your action across several days.
levels.practice_prompt: Do your chosen action on several days this week. Mark each day you did it.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a simple log of the days you completed it.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the log easy to read?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they show up on multiple days?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the action consistent each time?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Celebrate their most consistent stretch.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan when in my day I will do my small action.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I repeated my action several days. Ask me what helped me come back.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining how you stay consistent.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across multiple days.
levels.safety_note: A missed day is just a day. Returning matters more than a perfect run.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A log claiming days with no real action behind them does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain what helps you stay consistent.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences explaining one thing that helps you keep showing up, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most useful tip.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about what keeps me coming back to my action.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained my consistency habit. Ask me if it would help a beginner.
levels.next_step: Apply consistency to something that matters to you.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: There is no single right way to be consistent. Yours can be unique.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Streak-pressure advice that shames missed days does not count here.

levels.mastery_goal: Stay consistent on a goal that matters to you.
levels.practice_prompt: Apply your consistency habit to a meaningful goal for two weeks. Reflect on what you kept up and what slipped.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on a real goal over time.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you see the goal and effort?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they apply it over real time?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they reflect honestly on ups and downs?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest a way to keep it sustainable.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set a realistic consistency plan for a goal I care about.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I stayed consistent on a real goal. Ask me what worked and what I would adjust.
levels.next_step: Get ready to support someone building a habit.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Sustainable beats intense. It is fine to scale down to keep going.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Claiming consistency with no real evidence over time does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member build a doable routine.
levels.practice_prompt: Help a member who is struggling to stay consistent. Suggest one specific, gentle adjustment that worked for you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's real routine?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the adjustment realistic?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was supportive, not shaming.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my consistency habit into a gentle tip for someone struggling.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone with consistency. Ask me if I avoided shame and stayed kind.
levels.next_step: Keep supporting members who are building routines.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Never shame a missed day. Help them restart kindly.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Pressuring someone about broken streaks does not count as helping.

skills.description: Keep a simple record so you can see how far you have come.

levels.mastery_goal: Record one note about today's progress.
levels.practice_prompt: Write one sentence about something you did today toward a goal.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a one-sentence progress note.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they record real progress?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did it capture one concrete thing?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Encourage another note tomorrow.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me notice one bit of progress from today worth recording.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I recorded one progress note. Ask me if it felt good to see it written.
levels.next_step: Try recording a note on a few more days.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Small progress counts. There is no minimum size for a note.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: An empty or blank note does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Keep progress notes across several days.
levels.practice_prompt: Write a short progress note on several days. Keep them in one place.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a simple progress log of several days.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the log easy to read?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they track across multiple days?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the notes show real progress?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out a clear sign of growth.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set up a simple way to track a few days of progress.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tracked several days. Ask me what pattern I notice.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why tracking helps you.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across multiple days.
levels.safety_note: Gaps in your log are okay. Track what you can.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Backfilling fake entries does not count as tracking.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain how tracking helps you keep going.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how seeing your progress affects your motivation, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the clearest point.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how tracking changed how I feel about my progress.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained why tracking helps. Ask me if it rings true for me.
levels.next_step: Use tracking on a longer, meaningful goal.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Tracking is for you, not for comparison with others.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A definition with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Track a real goal long enough to see change.
levels.practice_prompt: Track progress on a meaningful goal for two weeks, then write what the record taught you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection based on your real progress log.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you see the goal and the record?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they track over real time?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the record reveal something useful?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest one thing to track next.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me decide what to track for a goal that matters over two weeks.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tracked a real goal. Ask me what surprised me in the record.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone set up tracking.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: If the record shows slow progress, that is still information, not failure.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A reflection with no underlying record does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member start a simple tracking habit.
levels.practice_prompt: Help a member who wants to track progress. Suggest one simple, specific way to record it that worked for you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's real goal?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the method simple and doable?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was encouraging.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my tracking method into a simple tip for a beginner.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone track progress. Ask me if my tip was kind and specific.
levels.next_step: Keep helping members see their own progress.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Keep it simple. A complicated system can discourage a beginner.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Suggesting a tool with no guidance on how to use it does not count.

skills.description: Come back kindly after a break, instead of giving up.

levels.mastery_goal: Restart after a missed day without self-blame.
levels.practice_prompt: Think of a habit you dropped. Restart it today with one small action and write one kind sentence to yourself about it.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a note of your restart action and your kind sentence.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what they restarted?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they take a real restart action?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the self-talk kind?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Affirm that coming back counts.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me restart a dropped habit with one small, kind step today.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I restarted after a miss. Ask me how it felt to come back.
levels.next_step: Practice restarting a couple more times this month.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Missing is part of every journey. Coming back is the real skill.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Harsh self-criticism with no restart action does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Practice recovering more than once.
levels.practice_prompt: Each time you miss something this week, restart with a small step and note how you came back.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit notes from at least two restarts.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they restart more than once?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they come back kindly each time?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out the smoothest comeback.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan how I will restart quickly the next time I miss.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I recovered more than once. Ask me what made restarting easier.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining how you bounce back.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice of recovering.
levels.safety_note: You are not behind. Every restart is a fresh, valid beginning.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Pretending you never missed does not count as recovering.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain how you bounce back after a miss.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how you recover after dropping a habit, with a real example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn to bounce back?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the most encouraging line.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how I get back on track after a break.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained how I bounce back. Ask me if it would comfort a beginner.
levels.next_step: Use your comeback plan on a goal you care about.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your comeback method is personal and valid.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Advice based on shame or punishment does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Recover from a real setback on a real goal.
levels.practice_prompt: After a real setback on a meaningful goal, use your comeback plan to keep going. Reflect on the recovery.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on recovering from a real setback.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you understand the setback and recovery?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they recover from something real?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they handle it kindly and keep going?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest a way to make recovery easier next time.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan how to recover from a setback on a goal I care about.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I recovered from a real setback. Ask me what I learned about myself.
levels.next_step: Get ready to support someone who feels they failed.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Setbacks are normal. If a goal stops fitting your life, adjusting it is also a valid recovery.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Claiming you recovered when you actually gave up does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member who feels like they failed.
levels.practice_prompt: Reply to a member who feels discouraged after missing. Offer one kind, specific way to restart that worked for you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the feedback you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the person's real feelings?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the person use the restart tip?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was warm and non-judging.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me comfort and encourage a member who feels they failed.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone restart. Ask me if I was kind and avoided shame.
levels.next_step: Keep welcoming members back after breaks.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Never call a miss a failure. Treat their setback the way you would want yours treated.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Guilt-tripping someone about quitting does not count as helping.

directions.description: Build the habit of looking honestly at your own thoughts, actions, and patterns, so you can grow with awareness.

skills.description: Name what you feel without judging it.

levels.mastery_goal: Name one feeling you had today.
levels.practice_prompt: Pick one moment today. Write the feeling you had and one thing that caused it.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short note naming one feeling and its cause.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what they felt?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they share something honest?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they connect a feeling to a cause?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back the feeling they named.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me find words for a feeling I had today. Offer a few options.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I named one feeling. Ask me if naming it changed anything.
levels.next_step: Try naming feelings on a few more days.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: All feelings are allowed here. You only share what feels safe to share.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Writing 'fine' with no real feeling named does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Name feelings across several days.
levels.practice_prompt: On several days, note one feeling and its cause. Keep them together.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short feelings log from several days.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the log easy to read?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they reflect across multiple days?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Were the feelings named honestly?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out a recurring feeling.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me make a simple way to note one feeling each day.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I logged feelings for several days. Ask me what I notice.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why naming feelings helps.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across multiple days.
levels.safety_note: Hard feelings are okay to note. If anything feels overwhelming, please reach out to someone you trust.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A log of vague one-word entries with no causes does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain why noticing feelings helps you.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how naming your feelings affects your choices, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the clearest insight.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how naming feelings has helped me decide things.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained why noticing feelings helps. Ask me if it matches my life.
levels.next_step: Use feeling-awareness in a real decision.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: AI can help you find words, but only you decide what your feelings mean.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Generic emotional advice with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use feeling-awareness to guide a real choice.
levels.practice_prompt: Before a real decision this week, name what you feel and let it inform your choice. Reflect on what happened.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on a real decision guided by your feelings.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use this in a real choice?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did noticing feelings help them decide?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest another moment to try it.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me check in with my feelings before a real decision I am facing.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I used my feelings in a real choice. Ask me what I learned.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone name their feelings.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Feelings inform choices but do not have to control them. Big decisions can wait until you feel steady.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A reflection with no real decision behind it does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member put words to a feeling.
levels.practice_prompt: Reply to a member sharing something hard. Gently help them name what they might be feeling, without telling them what to feel.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the supportive reply you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Was the reply easy to understand?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they respond to the person's real share?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did it help without labeling them?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was gentle and respectful.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me gently support a member to name their own feeling without deciding it for them.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone name a feeling. Ask me if I let them stay in control.
levels.next_step: Keep supporting members who are reflecting.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: You are a peer, not a therapist. For serious distress, kindly point them to professional help.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Telling someone how they should feel does not count as helping.

skills.description: Look back briefly to learn from how a day went.

levels.mastery_goal: Review one day in a few sentences.
levels.practice_prompt: At the end of today, write what went well and one thing you would change.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short end-of-day review.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they reflect honestly?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they note a win and a change?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back their win.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me two simple questions to review how my day went.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I reviewed my day. Ask me if anything stood out.
levels.next_step: Try a daily review a few more times.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Reviewing is for learning, not for judging yourself harshly.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Listing only criticism with no balance does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Review several days in a row-ish.
levels.practice_prompt: Do a short end-of-day review on several days. Keep them together.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit reviews from several days.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they review multiple days?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did each note a win and a change?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out a useful pattern.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set up a quick daily review I can keep up.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I reviewed several days. Ask me what shows up repeatedly.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why daily review helps.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across multiple days.
levels.safety_note: Skip a day if you need to. The habit matters more than perfection.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Copy-paste reviews with no real reflection do not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain what daily review does for you.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how reviewing your day changes your next day, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the best takeaway.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how reviewing days has helped me adjust.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained why review helps. Ask me if it feels true.
levels.next_step: Use review to improve a real, ongoing effort.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your reflection style is your own. Keep it kind.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A definition with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use reviews to adjust a real effort over time.
levels.practice_prompt: Review your days for two weeks while working on a goal, and use what you learn to adjust. Reflect on the changes you made.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection showing how reviews changed your actions.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they review over real time?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the reviews lead to real changes?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest one more thing to watch for.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me use my daily reviews to spot one thing to adjust on a goal.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I used reviews to adjust a real effort. Ask me what changed.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone start reviewing.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Adjusting based on reflection is wisdom, not failure.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A reflection with no real adjustments made does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member start reviewing their days.
levels.practice_prompt: Help a member who wants to reflect more. Share one simple, specific review question that worked for you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the suggestion you gave another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's goal?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was the review question simple to use?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was encouraging.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my review habit into one simple question for a beginner.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone start reviewing. Ask me if my tip was kind and clear.
levels.next_step: Keep helping members reflect well.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Encourage gentle reflection, not harsh self-grading.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A complicated reflection system dumped on a beginner does not count.

skills.description: Spot the habits and cycles that repeat in your life.

levels.mastery_goal: Notice one pattern in your behavior.
levels.practice_prompt: Look back at the past week. Write one thing you noticed yourself doing more than once.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a note naming one pattern you noticed.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you tell what pattern they saw?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they look back honestly?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Was it a real, repeated pattern?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back the pattern they named.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me look back at my week and spot one thing I repeated.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I named one pattern. Ask me if I had noticed it before.
levels.next_step: Try spotting patterns across a longer stretch.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Patterns are information, not judgments about who you are.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Naming a one-time event as a pattern does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Track patterns over a couple of weeks.
levels.practice_prompt: Over two weeks, note repeated behaviors or feelings you observe. List the patterns you find.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short list of patterns observed over time.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they observe over real time?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Were these genuine patterns?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out the strongest pattern.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set up a simple way to watch for repeated behaviors.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tracked patterns for two weeks. Ask me which surprised me.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining one pattern and its trigger.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across real time.
levels.safety_note: Notice patterns with curiosity, not criticism.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Guessing patterns without observing does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain one pattern and what triggers it.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences explaining one pattern you have, what tends to trigger it, and how it affects you.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation of a pattern and its trigger.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they connect pattern, trigger, and effect?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the clearest connection.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions to connect one of my patterns to what triggers it.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained a pattern and its trigger. Ask me if it feels accurate.
levels.next_step: Try changing how you respond to one trigger.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Understanding a pattern is enough at this stage. You do not have to fix it yet.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Naming a pattern with no link to a trigger or effect does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Change how you respond to one real pattern.
levels.practice_prompt: Choose one pattern you want to shift. Try a different response when its trigger appears, and reflect on what happened.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on changing a real pattern.

feedback_rubric.clarity: Could you understand the pattern and change?
feedback_rubric.effort: Did they try a real new response?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the change have an effect?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest how to keep the new response going.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan a different response for the next time a pattern's trigger shows up.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I tried changing a pattern. Ask me what worked and what was hard.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone spot their patterns.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: Change is slow. One different response is real progress, even if the pattern returns.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Saying you will change with no real attempt does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member notice one of their patterns.
levels.practice_prompt: Reply to a member's reflection and gently point out a possible pattern you notice, framed as a question.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit the reflective question you offered another member.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the person's real reflection?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could it help the person see something new?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Note whether it was gentle, not labeling.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn a pattern I notice in someone's reflection into a gentle question.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I helped someone spot a pattern. Ask me if I stayed curious, not judging.
levels.next_step: Keep helping members reflect with curiosity.
levels.trust_signal: Gave useful feedback that helped another member.
levels.safety_note: Offer patterns as questions, not diagnoses. Let them decide if it fits.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Labeling someone's character or 'analyzing' them does not count as helping.

skills.description: Decide on purpose how you want to show up, before you act.

levels.mastery_goal: Set one intention for your day.
levels.practice_prompt: Before your day really starts, write one short intention for how you want to show up today.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit one intention you set for the day.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they set a real intention?

feedback_rubric.next_step: Reflect back their intention.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set one short intention for how I want to show up today.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I set an intention. Ask me if I kept it in mind.
levels.next_step: Try setting an intention on a few more days.

levels.safety_note: An intention is a direction, not a strict rule. Missing it is okay.

levels.mastery_goal: Set and revisit intentions across days.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they set intentions on multiple days?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did they revisit them honestly?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Point out an intention that worked well.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me build a quick routine to set and check an intention each day.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I set intentions for several days. Ask me which ones stuck.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining how intentions guide you.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across multiple days.
levels.safety_note: Revisit kindly. An unmet intention is information, not a failure.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Setting intentions but never revisiting them does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain how intentions shape your actions.
levels.practice_prompt: Write 4-6 sentences on how setting intentions changes how you act, with an example.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a short explanation with an example.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could a beginner learn from it?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Name the clearest insight.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Ask me questions about how an intention changed one of my days.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I explained how intentions guide me. Ask me if it feels true.
levels.next_step: Set an intention for a real, important situation.
levels.trust_signal: Can explain the skill in their own words with an example.
levels.safety_note: Your intentions are personal. There is no correct one.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A definition with no personal example does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Set an intention before a real, important moment.
levels.practice_prompt: Before a real important event this week, set a clear intention for how you want to show up. Reflect on how it went.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit a reflection on an intention used in a real moment.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they set and use a real intention?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did the intention shape how they showed up?
feedback_rubric.next_step: Suggest another moment to try it.

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me set an intention for an important event coming up.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I used an intention in a real moment. Ask me if it helped me show up well.
levels.next_step: Get ready to help someone set their own intention.
levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.
levels.safety_note: An intention guides you; it does not guarantee an outcome. Be kind to yourself either way.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A reflection with no real event behind it does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member set a clear intention.

directions.description: Learn to ask questions that open up understanding, for others and for yourself.

skills.description: Ask to make sure you understood correctly.

levels.proof_requirement: Submit the clarifying question you asked.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they ask it in a real moment?

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Give me a simple template for a clarifying question I can use today.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I asked a clarifying question. Ask me how the other person responded.
levels.next_step: Try clarifying questions in a few more talks.
levels.trust_signal: Completed first practice and submitted proof.
levels.safety_note: Asking to understand is a strength, not a sign you missed something.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: A yes/no question that does not check meaning does not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Use clarifying questions several times.
levels.practice_prompt: In three conversations, ask a clarifying question before responding. Note each one.
levels.proof_requirement: Submit three clarifying questions from three conversations.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they practice across conversations?
feedback_rubric.usefulness: Did each one check understanding?

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me plan everyday moments to practice clarifying questions.
levels.ai_reflection_prompt: I clarified three times. Ask me if it reduced confusion.
levels.next_step: Move toward explaining why clarifying helps.
levels.trust_signal: Showed repeated practice across more than one conversation.
levels.safety_note: Record your own questions, not others' private details.
levels.does_not_count_as_mastery: Three versions of the same single question do not count.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain why clarifying prevents misunderstanding.

levels.trust_signal: Applied the skill in a real situation and reflected on it.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member ask clearer clarifying questions.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they engage with the member's situation?

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member ask more open questions.

levels.mastery_goal: Help a member ask better follow-ups.

feedback_rubric.usefulness: Could the member use the phrasing?

levels.ai_prep_prompt: Help me turn my follow-up habit into a tip for a beginner.

skills.description: Ask yourself honest questions to understand what you want.

levels.mastery_goal: Explain how self-questions clarify what you want.

feedback_rubric.effort: Did they use a real example?

directions.description: Learn to respond to others' work in a way that is kind, specific, and genuinely helpful.
